verdict: A well-synced, energetic chart that matches the robotic vibe of the track, but suffers from repetitive patterning and a few awkward high-speed transitions.

issues.problem: The constant repetition of JUMP LR -> tap U -> tap D -> tap L feels highly rigid and gets physically repetitive to play, missing opportunities for more varied choreography.
A 16th-note gallop from U to D at 160 BPM is extremely uncomfortable, requiring a fast double-step or slide across opposite panels which is too punishing for a Medium-level chart.
Tapping U and immediately transitioning into a JUMP LU creates an awkward double-tap on U during a jump, causing a hitch in player momentum.
Releasing HOLD U while hitting tap D at 31.791, followed by a 16th-note tap R at 31.884, requires a highly technical foot-swap/weight transfer that exceeds the expected difficulty.
